top_signals.title: BP Flags $1 Billion Writedown From Low Carbon Business
top_signals.summary: BP Plc said it expects to write down $1 billion from energy transition assets in the second quarter, as the British major continues the painstaking work of re-orientating itself toward its core oil and gas business.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: Middle East War Set to Push Back LNG Glut by a Year, BNEF Says
top_signals.summary: The liquefied natural gas market will move into oversupply in 2028, a year later than previously forecast, as war in the Middle East and major project delays push back the emergence of a global glut, according to BloombergNEF.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: Europe Is Set for the Strongest Earnings Growth in Three Years
top_signals.summary: European companies are set to post their strongest profit growth in three years in the second-quarter reporting season, buoyed by expected blowout results from oil majors, banks and artificial intelligence winners.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Commodity repricing impacting inflation expectations and industrial supply chains.

top_signals.title: Deutsche Bank and World Bank Team Up for Trade Finance Platform
top_signals.summary: The World Bank and Deutsche Bank AG are starting a €1 billion trade finance platform for frontier and emerging markets.
